# CodeOutbox

> Email marketing for developers. Paste a `<form>` (no backend), write broadcasts in
> Markdown, and operate it all from your coding agent over MCP. Lists and campaigns live in
> the repo as files. Free for 100 subscribers; open source and self-hostable.

CodeOutbox stores subscribers, handles double opt-in + consent, and sends email, so the
developer hosts nothing. Inbox-grade deliverability defaults (double opt-in, SPF/DKIM/DMARC,
one-click unsubscribe, suppression). To send broadcasts above the free-tier limit, the
account authenticates its own domain — the agent generates and verifies the DNS records.

## Capture (no backend)

Paste this; submissions become pending subscribers and get a double-opt-in email.
`{form_id}` is your list's unguessable public id (from `co groups` / the dashboard);
list slugs are namespaced per account, so the public id is what the form posts to:

```html
<form action="https://co.app/f/{form_id}" method="POST">
  <input type="email" name="email" required />
  <button>Subscribe</button>
</form>
```

- `POST /f/{form_id}` accepts `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` or `application/json`.
- Fields: `email` (required), optional `name`, hidden `_gotcha` (honeypot).
- `Accept: application/json` → `{ "ok": true, "status": "pending" }`; otherwise 303 redirect.

## Broadcasts (Markdown)

A campaign is YAML frontmatter + Markdown:

```markdown
---
subject: Launch week is here
group: newsletter
---
Hello — here's what shipped this week…
```

Send with the CLI or API: `co send campaigns/launch.md --live`.

## CLI (`co`)

- `co sync [codeoutbox.json]` — reconcile declared lists (config-as-code).
- `co send <file> [--live]` — preview (dry-run) or send a broadcast.
- `co domains add|verify|list <subdomain>` — authenticate a sending domain (SPF/DKIM/DMARC).
- `co token create [--name]` — create an API token.
- `co dev [--port]` — local form catcher for testing (no real emails).

Auth: `CO_TOKEN` (API token) as `Authorization: Bearer`. Server: `CO_URL`.

## MCP (agent control)

An MCP stdio server exposes: `list_groups`, `create_group`, `subscriber_count`,
`preview_broadcast`, `send_broadcast` (confirm-gated), `add_domain`, `verify_domain`,
`list_domains`. Configure via `.mcp.json` with `CO_URL` + `CO_TOKEN`.

## Sign up (instant)

`POST /signup { email }` → magic-link email → open it → you get an account + an API key
(shown once). Use the key as `Authorization: Bearer <key>` for the API below.

## Control-plane API (authd; Bearer token)

- `GET/POST /v1/groups` (lists), `GET /v1/groups/{slug}/count`
- **Subscribers (programmatic — push your SaaS's users):**
  - `POST /v1/groups/{list}/subscribers` `{ email, name?, confirmed? }`
    (`confirmed:true` asserts prior consent → no opt-in email)
  - `POST /v1/groups/{list}/subscribers/import` `{ subscribers:[...], confirmed? }`
  - `GET /v1/groups/{list}/subscribers`, `DELETE /v1/groups/{list}/subscribers/{email}`
- `POST /v1/broadcasts/preview`, `POST /v1/broadcasts` (requires `{ "confirm": true }`)
- `POST/GET /v1/domains`, `POST /v1/domains/{id}/verify`
- `GET/POST /v1/tokens`, `DELETE /v1/tokens/{id}`
- Public: `GET /badge/{group}` (subscriber-count SVG), `GET /confirm/{token}`,
  `GET|POST /unsubscribe/{token}`

## Pricing

Free (100 subscribers, ~1k sends/mo, shared subdomain) · Pro $9/mo (3,000, own domain) ·
Growth $19/mo (10,000) · Scale $49/mo (25,000). No credit card for free. Self-host is free.
